trace.rationale: Historical MLB data shows roughly 55% of first innings have at least one run scored, with no specific team or pitcher context to adjust this baseline.
Historical data shows roughly 50-55% chance of a run in the first inning across MLB games, with slight variation by teams and pitchers.

Historical MLB data shows roughly 50-55% of first innings have at least one run scored, with slight variation based on team strengths and starting pitchers. Without specific game details, a slight edge to 'Yes' is reasonable.
